channel.title: 2026-06-13 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-13 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: 三结果多体贝尔不等式：在多体系统维度判别和自旋向列挤压中的应用
item.description:
📚 npj Quantum Information
👤 Guillem Müller-Rigat, Albert Aloy, Maciej Lewenstein, Matteo Fadel, Jordi Tura
本研究探讨了三结果多体贝尔不等式及其在量子信息领域的应用。具体而言，该工作展示了这些不等式如何用于维度判别以及多体系统中的自旋向列挤压现象。这为理解和利用多体量子关联提供了新的理论工具和实验验证方向。

item.title: Between Mott and cluster Mott: spin-orbit entangled dimer singlets in Ba 3 CeRu 2 O 9
item.description:
📚 npj Quantum Materials
👤 Lara Pätzold, Anna Sandberg, Henrik Schilling, Hlynur Gretarsson, Enrico Bergamasco, Marco Magnaterra 等11位作者
npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 13 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00909-0 Between Mott and cluster Mott: spin-orbit entangled dimer singlets in Ba 3 CeRu 2 O 9

item.title: TEG-VRPTW: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Architecture with Temporal Edge-Gating and Amplitude-Encoded Quantum Bridge
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 kamel HOUARI, salim chikhi
The Vehicle Routing Problem with Time Windows(VRPTW) remains a fundamental challenge in combinatorialoptimization. Classical Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) struggleto capture temporal feasibility constraints due to isotropicattention mechanisms that are blind to time window structureduring message passing. The primary contribution of this work isa Temporal Edge-Gating (TEG) mechanism that explicitly modelsedge traversal feasibility via temporal slack, injecting a problemspecificinductive bias into GNN message passing. As a secondaryproof-of-concept, a Quantum Bridge (QB) uses Amplitude Encodingto compress 128-dimensional embeddings into 7 qubitsvia Variational Quantum Circuits (VQC), providing regimespecificrepresentational efficiency within NISQ constraints. Noasymptotic computational spee…

item.title: Bidirectional manipulation of gate-free quantum electronic states via semiconductor interface engineering
item.description:
📚 Physics News
A recent study published in Nature Communications demonstrates precise control over electron spatial arrangement in two directions simultaneously—without any applied voltage—through interface engineering between semimetal bismuth (Bi) thin films and two-dimensional semiconductor MoS₂.

item.title: Emergence of occupied and unoccupied electronic states driven by quantum charge fluctuations in electron doped cuprate superconductors
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Shin-ichiro Ideta, Hiroshi Yamaguchi, Yudai Miyai, Yuki Tsubota, Yogendra Kumar, Masashi Arita 等10位作者
The origin of electron–boson interactions is central to understanding high-Tc superconductivity in cuprates. While phonons and magnetic fluctuations are widely considered as candidates for mediating electron pairing, the role of charge fluctuations– one of the fundamental electronic degrees of freedom– remains unclear. Here, we investigate the electronic structure of the electron-doped cuprate Nd2−xCexCuO4 using angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy and angle-resolved inverse photoemission spectroscopy, which reveal the occupied and unoccupied states, respectively. We identify emergent spectral features on both occupied and unoccupied states that are consistent with excitations driven by quantum charge fluctuations. The results obtained in this study offer direct experimental insight…

item.title: Nuclear clocks tick for the first time
item.description:
📚 Physics News
Two independent research teams have achieved a longstanding goal in physics: building a working nuclear clock. The devices, developed by Beichen Huang and colleagues at Tsinghua University and by Luca Toscani De Col and colleagues at the Vienna Center for Quantum Science and Technology in Austria, exploit the nucleus of a thorium-229 atom to keep time with extraordinary precision—possibly surpassing even the best atomic clocks available today.

item.title: Revealing Relation of Surface Chemical State and Perovskite Quantum‐Dots Electroluminescent Performance
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Lin Zhang, Xiong Chen, Delin Kong, Chengyang Liang, Mengqi Zhang, Jing Li, Fei Huang, Jianjun Tian
Advanced Functional Materials, EarlyView.
